mapping.a_element: Molecular Dynamics simulations of ion interactions in neutron star crusts
mapping.b_element: Quantum Molecular Dynamics (QMD) simulations of nuclear pasta formation in supernovae

mapping.a_element: semi-analytical methods considering binary collisions of ions
mapping.b_element: transition process involving fusion of spherical nuclei into elongated structures

mapping.a_element: density-dependent behavior of shear viscosity in neutron star crusts
mapping.b_element: compression-driven structural phase transitions in supernova cores

brief_justification: While both papers employ Molecular Dynamics simulations to study dense nuclear matter, Paper A focuses on calculating shear viscosity via ion collisions and transport properties, whereas Paper B investigates structural phase transitions (e.g., lattice deformations) under compression. The core mechanisms—transport phenomena vs. morphological phase transitions—are fundamentally distinct.
